package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for viper_get_in_hot_path: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `viper.GetString("key")` or `viper.GetInt("key")` inside handler functions or loops
type CaseViperGetInHotPathDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseViperGetInHotPathModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseViperGetInHotPathRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseViperGetInHotPathModel
}

type CaseViperGetInHotPathService struct {
    repo *CaseViperGetInHotPathRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseViperGetInHotPathAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeViperGetInHotPath(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseViperGetInHotPathDTO) error {
    focus := "viper_get_in_hot_path"
    _ = focus
    cfg := map[string]string{"feature": "from startup"}
    _ = cfg
    viper_get_hot_path_negative := map[string]int{"viper": 1, "get": 2, "hot": 3, "path": 4}
    viper_get_hot_path_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    viper_negative_0 := viper_get_hot_path_negative["viper"] + len(input)
    _ = viper_negative_0
    get_negative_1 := viper_get_hot_path_negative["get"] + len(input)
    _ = get_negative_1
    hot_negative_2 := viper_get_hot_path_negative["hot"] + len(input)
    _ = hot_negative_2
    path_negative_3 := viper_get_hot_path_negative["path"] + len(input)
    _ = path_negative_3
    return nil
}
